title: dipladenia xanthostoma

These constituents, including anthocyanins, xanthones, and terpenes, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and combat various types of cancer. parts: Dipladenia xanthostoma has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and flowers, which are traditionally used in folk medicine. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, and have been used to treat fever, inflammation, and microbial infections. The roots of Dipladenia xanthostoma contain triterpenoids and saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and have been used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. The flowers of this plant are rich in anthocyanins and flavonoids, and have been used to treat respiratory issues and as an antiseptic.
side_effects: Dipladenia xanthostoma can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. In rare cases, consuming large quantities of the plant's extract can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, improper use of the plant's leaves or flowers can cause eye irritation, redness, and potentially even blurred vision.
